  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "An open 'what do you think?' over a research artifact centrally implicates the thesis — whether the problem framing, claimed gap (LLM vs dedicated NMT), and contribution (role-specialized debate + span arbitration) are coherently stated and mutually consistent.",
    "R1.2": "The paper makes a chain of inferential claims: multi-agent decomposition → arbitration policy → span-level quality → BLEU/COMET gains. Evaluating soundness requires checking whether premises (e.g., adequacy-threshold revision loop) logically support the stated conclusions, including the ablation logic.",
    "R1.3": "Several quantitative claims are made — +2.1/1.8/2.4 BLEU, ablation attributing 70% of gains to arbitration and 40% to terminology — without disclosed confidence intervals, significance tests, or comparison set details. Whether these numbers actually support the interpretive claims is a central axis of 'what do you think?'.",
    "R1.4": "The paper positions TransCollab against monolithic encoder-decoders and NLLB-3.3B but does not characterize the delta against prior multi-agent or LLM-ensemble MT work. Novelty assessment is a natural load for a general research opinion."
  },
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Are you primarily asking me to scrutinize whether the empirical results and ablation percentages are well-supported by the described methodology (evidence-claim alignment, R1.3 load-bearing), or to assess how novel TransCollab is relative to prior multi-agent and LLM-based translation systems (novelty assessment, R1.4 load-bearing)?"
}

rationale.R1.1: An open 'what do you think?' over a research artifact centrally implicates the thesis — whether the problem framing, claimed gap (LLM vs dedicated NMT), and contribution (role-specialized debate + span arbitration) are coherently stated and mutually consistent.
rationale.R1.2: The paper makes a chain of inferential claims: multi-agent decomposition → arbitration policy → span-level quality → BLEU/COMET gains. Evaluating soundness requires checking whether premises (e.g., adequacy-threshold revision loop) logically support the stated conclusions, including the ablation logic.
rationale.R1.3: Several quantitative claims are made — +2.1/1.8/2.4 BLEU, ablation attributing 70% of gains to arbitration and 40% to terminology — without disclosed confidence intervals, significance tests, or comparison set details. Whether these numbers actually support the interpretive claims is a central axis of 'what do you think?'.
rationale.R1.4: The paper positions TransCollab against monolithic encoder-decoders and NLLB-3.3B but does not characterize the delta against prior multi-agent or LLM-ensemble MT work. Novelty assessment is a natural load for a general research opinion.

parsed.clarification_question: Are you primarily asking me to scrutinize whether the empirical results and ablation percentages are well-supported by the described methodology (evidence-claim alignment, R1.3 load-bearing), or to assess how novel TransCollab is relative to prior multi-agent and LLM-based translation systems (novelty assessment, R1.4 load-bearing)?
